title: quercus robur var. tricuspidata
intro: Quercus robur var. tricuspidata, also known as the Chinese Pedunculate Oak, is a variant of the Quercus robur species, a type of deciduous tree native to China. The bark of Quercus robur var. tricuspidata contains tannins, which are known for their antimicrobial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory properties, making it a potential remedy for various health issues. The medicinal properties of Quercus robur var. tricuspidata have been traditionally used to lower cholesterol levels, reduce blood pressure, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, making it a valuable herb for cardiovascular and joint health. Quercus robur var. tricuspidata is commonly prepared as a decoction, tea, or tincture for its herbal remedies, and can be found in some traditional Chinese medicine formulas.
